Name: hardware_aware_quantization
Title: Hardware-Aware Quantization for Efficient Deep Learning Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-aware model compression techniques, we can significantly improve the efficiency of deep learning inference on commodity GPUs without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as SNIP, AMP-ViT, and EMQ have explored adaptive mixed-precision quantization and training-free proxies for mixed-precision quantization. However, these approaches focus on specific aspects of quantization and do not provide a comprehensive framework for hardware-aware quantization. Our proposal builds upon these works and aims to provide a more holistic approach to quantization, taking into account the underlying hardware architecture and its implications on inference efficiency.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to quantization, focusing on hardware-aware model compression techniques to improve the efficiency of deep learning inference on commodity GPUs. By leveraging kernel-level throughput engineering and profiling tools, we aim to develop a comprehensive framework for quantization that takes into account the underlying hardware architecture and its implications on inference efficiency. Our approach will involve a combination of adaptive mixed-precision quantization, training-free proxies, and hardware-aware model compression techniques to achieve significant improvements in inference efficiency without sacrificing accuracy.
Experiments: Implement and evaluate the proposed hardware-aware quantization framework on a range of deep learning models and datasets.
Compare the performance of the proposed framework with state-of-the-art quantization approaches, such as SNIP, AMP-ViT, and EMQ.
Conduct a thorough analysis of the hardware implications of the proposed framework, including kernel-level throughput, memory bandwidth utilization, and cache hierarchy optimization.
Evaluate the scalability of the proposed framework on multiple GPUs and driver versions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require significant modifications to existing deep learning models and training pipelines.
The hardware-aware approach may not be directly applicable to all types of deep learning models or hardware architectures.
The evaluation of the proposed framework may require significant computational resources and expertise in kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-aware model compression techniques.
